1.texto_original: Depois disto fez-me sair para fora, ao átrio exterior, que dá para o norte; e me levou às câmaras que estavam defronte do largo vazio, e que estavam defronte do edifício, do lado do norte.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Ezequiel 42:1

"Depois disto fez-me sair para fora, ao átrio exterior, que dá para o norte; e me levou às câmaras que estavam defronte do largo vazio, e que estavam defronte do edifício, do lado do norte."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Ezequiel 42:1

Philological Analysis

"Depois disto fez-me sair para fora, ao átrio exterior, que dá para o norte; e me levou às câmaras que estavam defronte do largo vazio, e que estavam defronte do edifício, do lado do norte."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

13.texto_original: Então me disse: As câmaras do norte, e as câmaras do sul, que estão diante do lugar separado, são câmaras santas, em que os sacerdotes que se chegam ao Senhor comerão as coisas santíssimas. Ali porão as coisas santíssimas, as ofertas de cereais, as ofertas pelo pecado, e as ofertas pela culpa; porque o lugar é santo.

14.texto_original: Quando os sacerdotes entrarem, não sairão do santuário para o átrio exterior, mas porão ali as suas vestiduras em que ministram, porque elas são santas; e vestir-se-ão doutras vestiduras, e assim se aproximarão do lugar pertencente ao povo.
14.normal:
Devotional Study of Ezequiel 42:14

"Quando os sacerdotes entrarem, não sairão do santuário para o átrio exterior, mas porão ali as suas vestiduras em que ministram, porque elas são santas; e vestir-se-ão doutras vestiduras, e assim se aproximarão do lugar pertencente ao povo."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Ezequiel 42:14

Philological Analysis

"Quando os sacerdotes entrarem, não sairão do santuário para o átrio exterior, mas porão ali as suas vestiduras em que ministram, porque elas são santas; e vestir-se-ão doutras vestiduras, e assim se aproximarão do lugar pertencente ao povo."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
14.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Quando os sacerdotes entrarem, não sairão do santuário para o átrio exterior, mas porão ali as suas vestiduras em que ministram, porque elas são santas; e vestir-se-ão doutras vestiduras, e assim se aproximarão do lugar pertencente ao povo."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

20.texto_original: Mediu-o pelos quatro lados. Havia um muro em redor, de quinhentas canas de comprimento, e quinhentas de largura, para fazer separação entre o santo e o profano.
20.normal:
Devotional Study of Ezequiel 42:20

"Mediu-o pelos quatro lados. Havia um muro em redor, de quinhentas canas de comprimento, e quinhentas de largura, para fazer separação entre o santo e o profano."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.
